candidates.item: Your job search shouldn't be harder than your actual job. One platform for the entire loop.
candidates.why_interesting: SKIP — this is the homepage OG/Twitter tagline. A polished marketing slogan: can only be parroted back, not reacted to. The concrete thing behind it is candidate #2 below.
candidates.recency_note: Pulled from live homepage HTML at time of step-1 research.

candidates.item: I'm building MantriAI - an AI career advisor for job seekers who are tired of juggling 6…
candidates.why_interesting: The specific framing that today's job search requires stitching together 6+ separate tools (job boards, JD analyzers, ATS, LinkedIn content tools, networking tools, interview prep tools) and MantriAI collapses all of them into one workflow. This is a concrete, countable pain-point claim — not a vague 'all-in-one' boast — and it's distinctive because most competitors attack only one or two of these stages, not the full loop.
candidates.recency_note: Live LinkedIn headline as of step-1 research (March–July 2026 window).

candidates.item: From a LinkedIn Post to an AI Product - What 6 Years of Watching Job Searches Break Taught Me
candidates.why_interesting: The product origin story is unusually specific and credible: the founder spent 6 years observing job-search failures before building MantriAI, and the spark was a LinkedIn post (not a VC pitch or a hackathon). That's a rare, concrete founding narrative — it signals genuine domain obsession rather than opportunistic AI pivoting.
candidates.recency_note: LinkedIn post title visible in search snippet, dated March 25, 2026.

candidates.item: He ran user acquisition across Meta, Google, and TikTok, managing monthly spend above $7M. He built our TikTok channel from zero with no existing playbook.
candidates.why_interesting: The founder (Abhinav Jain) comes from a hardcore paid-growth background at Pocket FM — $7M+/month in ad spend across three platforms — not a typical career-tech or HR-tech background. He also built Pocket FM's TikTok channel from scratch with no playbook. This is a non-obvious founder profile for a career-advice product: someone who actually ran massive user-acquisition machines, which makes the 'I watched job searches break' narrative more striking because he was simultaneously managing growth at scale.
candidates.recency_note: LinkedIn recommendation snippet visible in search results.

candidates.item: First hire at MantriAI. Looking for a Founder's Office Intern. 4 biggest perks: work directly with me, build things from scratch, use AI to fix real startup chaos, ₹25,000/month + Claude Pro.
candidates.why_interesting: The job post is candid and unpolished in a refreshing way — 'fix real startup chaos' is an honest acknowledgment of early-stage messiness rather than the usual startup-gloss. Offering Claude Pro as a perk (not just a salary) signals that AI tooling is central to how the team actually works, not just what they're selling. Also: this was the very first hire, suggesting an extremely early-stage team at time of research.
candidates.recency_note: Visible as a repost in search snippet, approximately 1 month before step-1 research.

candidates.item: Only filter: you should have tinkered with AI - workflows, bots, automations, prompt stacks, Notion/Sheets jugaad, anything.
candidates.why_interesting: The word 'jugaad' (Hindi for resourceful improvisation/hack) in a hiring post written in English is a small but telling signal: the founder is writing authentically for an India-native audience and values scrappiness over pedigree. 'Prompt stacks' alongside 'Notion/Sheets jugaad' is a blunt, specific skill bar — no euphemisms about 'AI-first mindset' — which is the opposite of how most startups write job posts.
candidates.recency_note: Same post as candidate #5; reposted ~1 month before step-1 research.
